title: Leetcode 759. Employee Free Time
shortDescription: Given each employee's list of working intervals, compute the finite time intervals when every employee is free by merging/combining overlapping busy intervals across multiple sorted lists and reporting the gaps; this typically uses interval-merge or sweep-line (heap) techniques to handle overlaps across employees.

Leetcode 759. Employee Free Time

Given each employee's list of working intervals, compute the finite time intervals when every employee is free by merging/combining overlapping busy intervals across multiple sorted lists and reporting the gaps; this typically uses interval-merge or sweep-line (heap) techniques to handle overlaps across employees.

Asked at:

P

Procore

Amazon

Google

Question Timeline

See when this question was last asked and where, including any notes left by other candidates.

Company

All Regions
Region

Late January, 2026

P

Procore

Senior

Late January, 2026

Amazon

Staff

Its a variant of employee free time, where instead of finding the free time given employee oncall timings, we have to find intervals where emloyees are oncall. for example, lets say user1 - [1,10], user2 - [5,7], user3-[6,11]
we have to give out like
1-5 = user1 oncall
5-6 = user1, user2 oncall
6-7 = user1, user2, user3 oncall
7-10 = user1, user3 oncall
10-11 = user3 oncall

we can use line sweep method by sorting the all start and end times for this by maintaining a set of current oncall and adding and removing the user from set when start or end time comes

Late December, 2024

Google

Employee Free Time

Mid September, 2024

Google

Junior

Given a struct Block representing busy time intervals for people and a total duration, find the time intervals during which all persons are free
